mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market expansion, which supports rising consumer income and corporate earnings, driving S&P 500 forward returns higher over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If payrolls decline over a 3-month period while SPX 20-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of cases, or if the rank correlation between monthly payroll changes and subsequent 20-day returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the year-over-year change in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 3%, it signals overheating and imminent monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in months where CPI 12-month change exceeds 3%, SPX 20-day returns are positive more than 55% of the time, or if the average excess return relative to low-inflation periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of strong retail sales growth (RSAFS, year-over-year > 5%) and an accommodative federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS < 1%) creates a 'sweet spot' for equity markets: robust consumer demand without restrictive borrowing costs, fueling positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: If the joint condition holds but SPX 20-day returns are positive in fewer than 60% of instances, or if the signal's predictive accuracy is not significantly better than the sum of individual contributions (interaction term p-value > 0.10 in a regression framework).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime, strong real GDP growth (GDPC1, quarter-over-quarter annualized above 3%) indicates robust economic momentum, boosting corporate profits and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If during post-recession periods with GDP growth above 3%, SPX 60-day returns are positive in less than 60% of cases, or if the average return is not significantly higher than in other regimes (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing activity and supply-side strength, which translates into higher corporate earnings and a favorable backdrop for S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: If month-over-month INDPRO increases are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or if the rolling 24-month rank correlation with forward returns is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above the 5.0% threshold signals labor market deterioration, weakening consumer spending and corporate profits, which depresses S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in months where UNRATE exceeds 5%, SPX 20-day returns are not significantly lower than the unconditional mean (one-sided t-test p > 0.05), or if the proportion of positive returns is above 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When both industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) exhibit concurrent month-over-month growth above 0.5% and 1%, respectively, it signals synchronized demand-supply expansion across manufacturing and construction, a strong positive for economic growth and S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND HOUST month-over-month growth > 1.0%
failure_condition: If the joint condition is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in less than 65% of cases, or if the interaction term in a regression including individual effects is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle (when the federal funds rate rises above 1.5% and is on an upward trajectory), each additional 25 basis point increase in FEDFUNDS raises borrowing costs and reduces equity risk appetite, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short one-week horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 1.5% and rising by at least 25bp over the prior quarter
failure_condition: If during tightening cycles FEDFUNDS increases are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or if the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).
